53e4fcc8-1f43-4600-ba6f-bd63456131cc.label: Bounded Set
53e4fcc8-1f43-4600-ba6f-bd63456131cc.description:
\documentclass{article}
\begin{document}
  
  Let $X$ be a \nodelink{metric space}{metric space} with \nodelink{metric}{metric} $d$. A subset $A$ of $X$ is said to be \emph{bounded} if there is some number $M$ such that
  \[
    d(a_{1},a_{2}) \le M
  \]
  for every pair $a_{1}, a_{2}$ of points of $A$.

  If $A$ is bounded and nonempty, the \emph{diameter} of $A$ is defined to be the number
  \[
    \operatorname{diam} A = \sup \{\, d(a_{1},a_{2}) \mid a_{1}, a_{2} \in A \,\}.
  \]
  
\end{document}

ba010cf8-42df-420e-b0cb-59a3310ae9b6.label: Norm and Standard Metrics
ba010cf8-42df-420e-b0cb-59a3310ae9b6.description:
\documentclass{article}
\begin{document}
  
  Given $x = (x_{1}, \ldots, x_{n})$ in $\mathbb{R}^{n}$, we define the following \nodelink{metrics}{metrics}:
  
  We define the \emph{norm} of $x$ by the equation
  \[
    \Vert x \Vert = (x_{1}^{2} + \cdots + x_{n}^{2})^{1/2}.
  \]

  We define the \emph{euclidean metric} $d$ on $\mathbb{R}^{n}$ by the equation
  \[
    d(x,y) = \Vert x - y \Vert = \bigl[(x_{1} - y_{1})^{2} + \cdots + (x_{n} - y_{n})^{2}\bigr]^{1/2}.
  \]

  We define the \emph{square metric} $\rho$ by the equation
  \[
    \rho(x,y) = \max \{\, |x_{1} - y_{1}|, \ldots, |x_{n} - y_{n}| \,\}.
  \]
  
\end{document}
